proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_012 (a continuous variable, range 30-90 — appears age-like) is positively associated with longer pfs_months across the full cohort.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df.feature_012, df.pfs_months); smf.ols('pfs_months~feature_012').fit()
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.702 (p=<1e-300); univariate OLS slope = +0.1759 mo per unit of feature_012 (p=<1e-300). feature_012 is by far the strongest single predictor of pfs_months in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months decreases monotonically across the three ordered levels of feature_005 (level 0 highest, level 2 lowest), consistent with feature_005 being a stage-/severity-like ordinal predictor.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df.feature_005==v,'pfs_months'] for v in [0,1,2]])
analyses.result_summary: Means: level 0 = 5.64 (n=17592); level 1 = 4.44 (n=24971); level 2 = 3.29 (n=7437). ANOVA F p=<1e-300. Level 2 vs level 0 = -2.35 months. Strict monotonic decrease confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among binary features, feature_006=1 and feature_001=1 are strongly associated with SHORTER pfs_months (adverse markers).
Among binary features, feature_013=1 is strongly associated with LONGER pfs_months — a candidate therapeutic intervention or favorable predictive marker.
Approximately half of the binary features (e.g., feature_011, feature_030, feature_019, feature_032, feature_009) have no detectable association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: for c in binary: ttest_ind(pfs[c==1], pfs[c==0])
analyses.result_summary: Largest effects: feature_006: diff=-1.536 (p=<1e-300); feature_001: diff=-1.050 (p=1.45e-178); feature_028: diff=-0.559 (p=6.07e-144); feature_013: diff=+1.098 (p=<1e-300); feature_029: diff=+0.568 (p=3.70e-143); feature_023: diff=+0.362 (p=5.82e-59). feature_006 (-1.54 mo, prev 30%) and feature_001 (-1.05 mo, prev 10%) are adverse. feature_013 (+1.10 mo, prev 35%) is favorable. feature_029 (+0.57), feature_028 (-0.56), feature_018 (-0.46), feature_023 (+0.36) are intermediate.

analyses.code: for c in binary: ttest_ind ; filter |diff|<0.1
analyses.result_summary: Null binary features (|diff| < 0.1 mo): feature_020: diff=-0.0741 (p=1.75e-02); feature_022: diff=-0.0605 (p=2.92e-02); feature_015: diff=+0.0431 (p=7.69e-02); feature_008: diff=-0.0363 (p=3.30e-01); feature_009: diff=-0.0245 (p=7.24e-01). Eight of 18 binary features show no detectable PFS association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond feature_012, the continuous variables feature_004 (negative), feature_024 (positive), and feature_033 (negative) are the next strongest correlates of pfs_months.

The remaining continuous features (lab-like values: feature_002, feature_007, feature_010, feature_025, feature_017, feature_034, feature_014, feature_021, feature_003, feature_036, feature_035, feature_037, feature_027, feature_031) are essentially uncorrelated with pfs_months at clinically meaningful magnitudes (|r| < 0.02 except feature_031 ~ -0.02).

analyses.code: for c in continuous: pearsonr(c, pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Top continuous correlations: feature_012: r=+0.702 (p=<1e-300); feature_004: r=-0.116 (p=4.07e-148); feature_024: r=+0.100 (p=9.39e-112); feature_033: r=-0.091 (p=2.98e-93); feature_031: r=-0.016 (p=2.45e-04); feature_027: r=-0.013 (p=2.95e-03). After feature_012, the next-strongest are feature_004 (-0.116), feature_024 (+0.100), feature_033 (-0.091); 14 other continuous features have |r|<0.02.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A linear multivariable model containing all 37 features explains a large share of pfs_months variance (R^2 >= 0.7).

Adjusted for all other features, the strongest signed effects on pfs_months are: positive feature_012 and feature_013, feature_024; negative feature_006, feature_005, feature_001, feature_028, feature_018, feature_004, feature_033.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(pfs, sm.add_constant(df[all_features])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full multivariable OLS R^2 = 0.7923 (overall F p=<1e-300). Strongest adjusted predictors: feature_005: b=-1.1715 (p=<1e-300); feature_033: b=-0.0189 (p=<1e-300); feature_013: b=+1.1011 (p=<1e-300); feature_024: b=+0.4755 (p=<1e-300); feature_028: b=-0.5815 (p=<1e-300); feature_004: b=-0.0768 (p=<1e-300); feature_012: b=+0.1757 (p=<1e-300); feature_001: b=-0.9833 (p=<1e-300); feature_006: b=-1.5488 (p=<1e-300); feature_029: b=+0.5316 (p=8.63e-275); feature_018: b=-0.4708 (p=2.41e-239); feature_031: b=-0.0005 (p=2.21e-16). The 14 lab-like continuous features add essentially no predictive power.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong binary PFS predictors (feature_006, feature_001, feature_013, feature_028, feature_029, feature_023, feature_018, feature_016) are mostly approximately independent of each other; the largest co-occurrence is between feature_029 and feature_023.

analyses.code: df[strong_bin].corr().abs(); top pairwise |r|
analyses.result_summary: Top pairwise correlations: feature_029-feature_023: |r|=0.669; feature_018-feature_016: |r|=0.361; feature_028-feature_016: |r|=0.007. feature_029 and feature_023 are correlated |r|=0.669 (consistent with a hormone-receptor pair, e.g., ER+/PR+). All other pairs |r| <= 0.36, with the next-largest being feature_018 vs feature_016. Most pairs are near zero (median |r| ~0.005), suggesting near-independent biomarker generation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006=1 retains a strongly negative adjusted effect on pfs_months after controlling for feature_012, feature_005, feature_004, feature_024, feature_033.
feature_001=1 retains a strongly negative adjusted effect on pfs_months after the same adjustment.
feature_013=1 retains a strongly positive adjusted effect.
feature_028=1 and feature_018=1 retain negative adjusted effects; feature_029=1 retains a positive adjusted effect.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_006) + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033').fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=1 adjusted effect = -1.552 mo (p=<1e-300).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_001) + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033').fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_001=1 adjusted effect = -0.994 mo (p=<1e-300).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_013) + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033').fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 adjusted effect = +1.105 mo (p=<1e-300).

analyses.code: individual adjusted models

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment, higher feature_024 is associated with LONGER pfs_months.
After adjustment, higher feature_004 is associated with SHORTER pfs_months.
After adjustment, higher feature_033 is associated with SHORTER pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_024 + covars
analyses.result_summary: feature_024 adjusted slope = +0.4826 mo per unit (p=<1e-300). Range: 1.5-5.5 (~4 unit span ~ +1.9 mo).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_004 + covars
analyses.result_summary: feature_004 adjusted slope = -0.0771 mo per unit (p=<1e-300).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ feature_033 + covars
analyses.result_summary: feature_033 adjusted slope = -0.0186 mo per unit (p=3.51e-293).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 has many (>=3) strong (p<1e-10) two-way interactions with other binary features on pfs_months — consistent with feature_013 being a treatment whose efficacy is biomarker-modified.
feature_006 and feature_001, despite their strong main effects, have ZERO strong (p<1e-10) interactions with other binary features — consistent with being pure prognostic biomarkers, not treatments.

feature_028, feature_018, feature_029, feature_023 also show several strong interactions, all involving feature_013 — they are effect-modifiers of the feature_013 treatment rather than independent treatments.

analyses.code: for treat,col in candidates x binary: interaction p; count p<1e-10
analyses.result_summary: Strong-interaction counts (p<1e-10): feature_013: n_strong=5; feature_006: n_strong=0; feature_001: n_strong=0; feature_028: n_strong=4; feature_018: n_strong=4; feature_029: n_strong=3; feature_023: n_strong=3; feature_016: n_strong=1. feature_013 has 5 strong partners (top 3: feature_028(b=-1.62), feature_029(b=+1.59), feature_018(b=-1.36)). feature_006 and feature_001 have 0 strong partners — pure prognostic. feature_028/feature_018/feature_029/feature_023's strong partners all include feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the continuous features, feature_033 is by far the strongest modifier of the feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months: higher feature_033 attenuates the feature_013 benefit (negative interaction).

analyses.code: for c in continuous: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013*c').fit()
analyses.result_summary: Top continuous modifiers of feature_013: feature_033: b=-0.05240 (p=1.48e-174); feature_010: b=-0.09484 (p=3.79e-02); feature_012: b=-0.00323 (p=4.38e-02); feature_004: b=+0.01106 (p=6.04e-02). feature_033 dominates (p=1.48e-174); the next-best continuous interaction is ~7 orders of magnitude weaker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_013 effect on pfs_months is essentially null when feature_029=0 and substantial when feature_029=1 (positive interaction of large magnitude).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_029').fit(); stratified mean diffs
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_013:feature_029 = +1.594 (p=9.31e-229). Stratified mean PFS difference (treated - untreated): feature_029=0 stratum = -0.0175 mo (n=15114); feature_029=1 stratum = +1.5770 mo (n=34886).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018=1 abolishes the feature_013 PFS benefit (large negative interaction). feature_018 acts as a treatment-resistance / contraindication marker.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_018').fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_013:feature_018 = -1.364 (p=2.19e-118). Stratified diff: feature_018=0 = +1.3496 mo; feature_018=1 = -0.0141 mo. Effect collapses to zero when feature_018=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028=1 abolishes the feature_013 PFS benefit (large negative interaction). feature_028 is a second resistance/contraindication marker.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_028').fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_013:feature_028 = -1.623 (p=4.00e-255). Stratified diff: feature_028=0 = +1.6591 mo; feature_028=1 = +0.0361 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006, feature_001, and feature_005 do NOT modify the feature_013 treatment effect (interactions all non-significant). They are pure prognostic markers, not predictive biomarkers for feature_013.

analyses.code: individual interaction models with each prognostic feature
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 x feature_006: b=+0.0060 (p=9.01e-01); feature_013 x feature_001: b=-0.0410 (p=5.86e-01); feature_013 x feature_005[T.1]: b=-0.0212 (p=6.57e-01); feature_013 x feature_005[T.2]: b=+0.0346 (p=6.04e-01). All p>>0.05 — these features predict outcome but do NOT predict treatment response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint subgroup feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0, feature_013 produces a large pfs_months gain (>=2 months unadjusted, p<<0.05).
Outside that 3-condition subgroup, feature_013 has effectively zero effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: df.query('feature_029==1 & feature_018==0 & feature_028==0'); ttest by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: In 3-condition subgroup (n=18681, 37.4% of cohort): treated mean PFS = 7.209, untreated = 4.300; diff = +2.908 mo (p=<1e-300).

analyses.code: df.query('not (feature_029==1 & feature_018==0 & feature_028==0)'); ttest by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: Outside subgroup (n=31319): diff = +0.0028 mo (p=9.10e-01). Effectively zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A saturated 4-way interaction model on feature_013 x feature_029 x feature_018 x feature_028 places the entire feature_013 PFS benefit in the cell where feature_029=1, feature_018=0, feature_028=0, with two large negative 3-way interactions cancelling the effect when feature_018 OR feature_028 turn on.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_029 * feature_018 * feature_028').fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 = -0.042 (p=4.33e-01); feature_013:feature_029 = +2.950 (p=<1e-300); feature_013:feature_018 = -0.033 (p=7.94e-01); feature_013:feature_028 = +0.121 (p=1.86e-01); feature_013:feature_029:feature_018 = -2.892 (p=3.42e-81); feature_013:feature_029:feature_028 = -3.007 (p=2.13e-165); feature_013:feature_029:feature_018:feature_028 = +3.165 (p=6.08e-36). Pattern: ~+3 mo treatment effect appears only in (feature_029=1, feature_018=0, feature_028=0); 3-way interactions feature_013:feature_029:feature_018 ~ -2.9 and feature_013:feature_029:feature_028 ~ -3.0 each cancel the benefit when the second modifier turns on.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 3-condition subgroup, the feature_013 effect shows a sharp feature_033 threshold near 14: below it the effect is large (~+5 mo) and stable; above ~14 the effect collapses to ~0 within a couple of units.

analyses.code: for thr in cutoffs: ttest pfs by feature_013 within mask3 & feature_033<=thr

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 4-condition subgroup feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=14, feature_013 increases pfs_months by ~+5 months (both unadjusted and after covariate adjustment).
Outside that 4-condition subgroup (failing any one of the four conditions), feature_013 has zero effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 + covars', df[mask4]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Inside refined subgroup (n=10828): unadjusted treated mean = 9.228, untreated mean = 4.285, unadjusted diff = +4.943 mo. Adjusted feature_013 effect = +4.977 mo (p=<1e-300).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 + covars', df[~mask4]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Outside refined subgroup (n=39172): unadjusted diff = +0.0071 mo. Adjusted feature_013 effect = -0.0007 mo (p=7.45e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 4-condition refined subgroup (n=10828), no additional binary or continuous feature significantly modifies the feature_013 effect at p<1e-3 — the subgroup definition is COMPLETE.

analyses.code: loop interactions feature_013*X over all features within df[mask4], threshold p<1e-3
analyses.result_summary: Within refined subgroup (n=10828): 0 additional binary modifiers and 0 additional continuous modifiers at p<1e-3. The 4-condition subgroup is complete; no residual heterogeneity in the feature_013 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 and feature_016 do NOT need to be added to the subgroup definition: within the refined subgroup, the feature_013 effect (~+5 mo) is essentially identical across feature_023 and feature_016 strata.

analyses.code: stratify d4_in by feature_023 / feature_016; estimate feature_013 effect within each stratum
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=0: eff=+4.627 (p=7.18e-242, n=1599); feature_023=1: eff=+4.997 (p=<1e-300). feature_016=0: eff=+4.878 (p=<1e-300); feature_016=1: eff=+5.022 (p=<1e-300). Differences between strata are <0.4 mo and well within sampling variability.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative main effect of feature_028 on pfs_months is NOT a direct outcome harm: within feature_013=0 patients (in the otherwise eligible group) feature_028 has zero effect; within feature_013=1 patients feature_028=1 erases the treatment benefit (~-2.9 mo).

analyses.code: stratify by feature_013 within feature_018=0 & feature_029=1; estimate feature_028 effect
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_018=0 & feature_029=1: feature_013=0 stratum: feature_028 effect = +0.034 (p=2.94e-01); feature_013=1 stratum: feature_028 effect = -2.852 (p=<1e-300). Confirms feature_028's apparent harm is treatment-resistance, not independent prognostic injury.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mirror image: the negative main effect of feature_018 is also a treatment-resistance phenomenon, not a direct outcome harm — feature_018 has zero effect on pfs_months in feature_013=0 patients and ~-2.9 mo in feature_013=1 patients.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_013 within feature_028=0 & feature_029=1; estimate feature_018 effect
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_028=0 & feature_029=1: feature_013=0 stratum: feature_018 effect = +0.023 (p=6.16e-01); feature_013=1 stratum: feature_018 effect = -2.902 (p=3.62e-221). Same erasure pattern as feature_028.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Independent of feature_013, the worst-prognosis subgroup is feature_005=2 AND feature_006=1 AND feature_001=1: these patients have substantially shorter pfs_months than everyone else, reflecting compounded prognostic damage (none of these features modify treatment).

analyses.code: df.query('feature_005==2 & feature_006==1 & feature_001==1'); compare PFS to rest
analyses.result_summary: Worst-prognosis subgroup n=247, mean PFS = 1.537 vs rest n=49753, mean = 4.709; diff = -3.172 mo (p=6.20e-72). Confirms compounding of prognostic-only features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A fully-saturated interaction model among the four modifiers (feature_013 x feature_029 x feature_018 x feature_028 x feature_033) reproduces the same picture: predicted feature_013 contrast is large (~+5 mo) inside the subgroup and ~0 outside.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_029 * feature_018 * feature_028 * feature_033').fit(); predict in/out
analyses.result_summary: Predicted feature_013 contrast at one in-subgroup row = +3.251 mo; at one out-subgroup row = +0.091 mo. Saturated model is consistent with the simpler stratified estimates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis (best supported by the data): feature_013 increases pfs_months ONLY in patients meeting all four conditions: feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=14. Within this subgroup the adjusted pfs_months difference is approximately +4.98 months (treated vs untreated, p<<1e-300, n=10828). In every patient who fails any one of these four conditions the effect is approximately 0 (n=39172, p=0.73). feature_006, feature_001, feature_005 (and other prognostic features feature_012/024/004) DO predict pfs_months but do NOT modify the feature_013 treatment effect.

analyses.code: df[mask4].groupby('feature_013')['pfs_months'].mean(); df[~mask4].groupby('feature_013')['pfs_months'].mean(); adjusted OLS within each
analyses.result_summary: Inside refined subgroup (n=10828): treated mean pfs_months = 9.228, untreated = 4.285, unadjusted diff = +4.943 mo; adjusted = +4.977 mo (p=<1e-300). Outside subgroup (n=39172): treated mean = 4.324, untreated = 4.317, unadjusted diff = +0.007 mo; adjusted = -0.0007 mo (p=7.45e-01). feature_013 PFS benefit is fully concentrated in the subgroup feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=14.
